clean_text: , come on. Lord, Alhamdulillah. forch. Muslim. for Wasam. for Allah Subhanahu Waaala. Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala tells us in describing his truly believing servants, those from amongst his servants that will inherit the paradise. And not just any part of the paradise but they will inherit the highest levels of paradise. El ferdos he tells us subhanahu wa ta'ala describing them or we should say describing one of their most critical components and hallmarks. He says subhanahu waala and those who are ever dutiful in giving their zaka the zakiah of their wealth. He elaborates on this elsewhere in which he describes his believing servants. He describes elus. He describes those whom their hearts have been chosen and prepared by Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala to be filled with his revelation and his names and attributes and to be filled with knowledge of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. He says in their description and those whom in their wealth is a known and set a known and set right that belongs to those who ask and that belongs to those who are in desperate need. So Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala, notice the wording here. He says, notice the wording that he uses. He says, "And those whom in their wealth exists a right, a portion of their wealth, that is a right that belongs to those who are less privileged to those who are impoverished and those who are needy and are asking. This a known set right. This is what we know as zak. It is a known and set right that is contained within the wealthy individual's property and wealth. But it belongs to those who are impoverished. It belongs to those who are in need, those who are in a position to ask others for aid and help and financial assistance. And so this gives us a very interesting view of Zak. It gives us Zak in almost a new light from a different angle. And that is that when the person gives zak, he is actually handing over what never belonged to him in the first place. This is a right that Allah mentions belongs to those who are impoverished. It is a right that is contained within his wealth that belongs to those who are in need. And so Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala mentions that this known and set right belongs to those individuals who are in need of it. And when a person accepts Islam, when a person stands before Allah as a servant and a follower of Muhammad sallall.aii alaihi wasallam. He accepts and understands that he is merely the custodian of said wealth. That this portion of his earnings, though they are a result of his earnings, they are ultimately all the consequences of Allah's grace and mercy and giving. and that Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has allowed us the majority of our wealth but he has set aside 2.5% a very very small percentage that will not harm the wealthy but will be an immense benefit and of consequence to those who do not have and so it is a balance and Allah has said to the wealthy, "This is yours and it belongs to you. I hereby grant you this being that you have earned it in a hal way. But this portion right here belongs to me. And I have made you its custodian and its caretaker. And that you are to deliver it to eight types of individuals whom I have designated be recipients of this zaka. And Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has made that a pillar of Islam. And he has made it one of the first things that any people will learn about Islam. And Bkari Muslim Abdullahb he narrates that when the prophet Sam sent Mu Jabal to Yemen as his representative and a caller to Islam and judge amongst the people of Yemen. He told him The prophet sallall.aii Wasam when he sent Mu to Yemen, he said when you arrive in Yemen, you will encounter a people there that are a people of the book, predominantly Christian, but there were some pockets of Jews there as well. And so the first thing you are to call them to, the first thing you are to introduce them to of my message is that they worship Allah alone without any partners. He said, "And if they come to accept that, then inform them that Allah whom they have conceded is to be worshiped alone without any partners. Inform them that Allah has obligated upon them five daily prayers in every day and night of theirs. He said, "And if they come to accept this and they come to practice prayer, then inform them that Allah has obligated upon them a percentage of the wealth of their wealthy, a charity that will be taken from their wealthy and distributed back amongst their poor. Now notice the very critical wording here that the prophet sallall.aii wasallam cites. He says inform them that Allah has obligated upon them. Who is them? The people of Yemen. the people that he's going to people he's been sent to by the prophet sallall.aii wasallam inform them that Allah has obligated upon them a charity to be taken from their wealthy and distributed back upon their poor. The scholars have taken from this narration, this wording and other narrations that zaka is not only to be collected in a place but it is to be distributed in the place in which it was collected because zakia has many many many benefits. and a person receiving a check or a person receiving some money. That is only one of its many multiaceted benefits. One of the most important aspects and angles of zaka is that it strengthens the bonds and closes the chasm and gaps between the wealthy and the poor. Throughout human history, there has always existed a tension and a lovehate relationship between the wealthy and the poor. The poor need the wealthy. And the wealthy, they need the poor, whether they accept that or not. And the poor hate that they need the wealthy. And the wealthy hate that they need the poor. But Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has set us all up as he has said that he subhanahu wa ta'ala has raised some amongst us in levels above others in order for the test that is this world to be adequate to judge a person's true true person. And so there is always a possible there is always a possible vendetta or possible discomfort. And there is also always this kind of dislike that bubbles underneath the surface between the poor and the rich. And so one of the benefits, one of the most critical and immense benefits of zak is that it helps to quell that that dislike. It helps to quell that when the wealthy give enough to the poor to take care of them. And the wealthy give enough to the poor to make them productive in society in their society. And where the poor receive enough to make them thankful to the wealthy. And this is all part of a functioning society. If we have very very very wealthy people who don't look twice at the poor, this chasm will continue to grow between them until it becomes combustible. And so Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has made it that the wealthy must give the poor and the poor must be grateful to the wealthy in order that they all be brothers. And so this is a timeless law. This is a timeless legislation of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala that the wealthy in any given place or society are always responsible for taking care of the poor in that society here in America right here. If the wealthy amongst us do not take care of the poor and the wealthy amongst us ship their zaka off to some other place in the world and then they will say well you know hey the government takes care of the poor here in America so we don't really need to do that and so we're okay with the government quote unquote taking care of our brothers and sisters who are our brothers and sisters. BUT THEN WE SHIP OUR zak off somewhere else. How exactly are we going to grow those bonds of brotherhood between the wealthy and the poor amongst us? Or is our time too advanced and too complex for these kinds of simple these kinds of simple solutions? Of course not. This is the law of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. The sunnah of the prophet Sam. They are timeless. They are not bound by any geographic. These laws of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala are beneficial and we are the first to reap the benefits of these of these laws and these rules and regulations of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala these obligations and so it is absolutely critical that we take care of our poor. Sometimes I have heard some of the most absurd arguments with respect to people discussing this issue. People that claim that there's no poor people in America or for instance the government will take care of the poor or we have welfare here in America or all these other excuses. This money does not belong to you. This money that is zaka in your wealth, it never belonged to you. Never. It was never part of that which was okay for you to take. It always belonged to the poor. And it belonged to your poor brother and sister that lives right next door. And if he doesn't live right next door, then two doors down and so forth. And if he doesn't if there is hypothetically not a single poor person in your whole city than the next poor person in the closest city to you. This is how zaka works. You don't make your own rules for this kind of money. It is something that you are merely a custodian of. And it is absolutely critical for us to go back to those original original obligations and really start back at the fundamentals again and grow our roots nice and strong again. and that we start to give our zakat properly to who it belongs to. And if a person is unable to give it to who he belongs to, then he will give it to someone who will make sure that it arrives in the hands of those who are needy, those who are impoverished, those to whom it belongs. summary: This is a khutbah (sermon) delivered at the Muslim Community Center of Tucson focusing on the Islamic obligation of zakat (alms-giving). The speaker explains zakat as a fundamental pillar of Islam, emphasizing that it is not charity but a fixed right belonging to the poor that exists within the wealth of those who possess means. The sermon discusses how zakat serves to strengthen bonds between rich and poor, drawing from Quranic verses and hadith about the Prophet Muhammad's instructions to Muadh ibn Jabal when sending him to Yemen. The speaker stresses the importance of distributing zakat locally within one's community rather than sending it elsewhere, using America as an example of where wealthy Muslims should support their poor neighbors.
suggested_title: The Obligation of Zakat: A Right of the Poor, Not Charity from the Rich
hadith_chapter: Book on Zakat - Narration regarding Muadh ibn Jabal's mission to Yemen
